_comment: Model classes decoded from the enemy's embedded model SKELETON (docs/dc1/STATIC-SCD-RE.md cont.14/20-23). An enemy record's model pointer targets a model block in the same decompressed RDT buffer; that block's bone count (dword @ +0x14) plus bone parent-hierarchy is a stable per-model-class invariant. The skeleton topology is 1:1 with the AI category byte 'id' (record[2]). NOTE (cont.23): model class is NOT the same as biological species — DC1's single large theropod (the Tyrannosaurus) ships as TWO model classes (id 3 / 20 bones and id 4 / 10 bones), so 'id' is the AI category and is no longer equal to the DinoSpecies enum value. SOURCE OF TRUTH PAIRING: this table is locked to the C# code by EnemySpeciesTableTests — for every row FromBoneCount(bones) == SpeciesForCategory(id) (the two witnesses agree), and every DinoSpecies enum value appears at least once. Edit both together, or the test fails.

species.name: Velociraptor
species.note: standard raptor; the workhorse enemy (33 rooms). FAQ 'Raptor'. Same-category models are pose/mesh variants of this one species.

species.name: Raptor (heavy rig)
species.note: distinct raptor-class skeleton, heaviest rig; rooms 0109/010D/0308/030A/0401 (FAQ still calls these 'Raptor'). LIVE-CONFIRMED as the stage-5 'Blue Raptor' (docs cont.26): the 0511 spawn read entity+0x27=2 (category) AND model+0x14=21 (bone count) — both witnesses agree. Live combat test (0511): it dies normally and sets its group-4 kill-flag (killid 55, bank 0x6DD994 bit23) and its AI goes to the death state — a normal cat2 RaptorHeavy, just handgun-tanky; 'Blue' is palette/flavor only, NOT immune or a distinct rig. NB entity+0x167 is NOT current HP (stayed 30 through all damage AND death — the old 'HP candidate' is wrong); real HP field still unlocated.

species.name: Tyrannosaurus (boss rig)
species.note: DC1's large theropod. 20-bone AI-cat-3 boss model, the largest skeleton in the game; placed as a scripted set-piece in every 'T-Rex room' 0201/0403/0600/060A/060C/0611/0613. SAME species as the 10-bone cat-4 rig below (cont.23) — they are two model classes of one animal, so 'id' (category) is no longer the DinoSpecies enum value. Was mislabeled 'Large ground dino' until the roster/full-corpus census cross-check identified it as the Tyrannosaurus.

species.name: Tyrannosaurus (Chief's Room rig)
species.note: Second T-Rex model class. 10-bone AI-cat-4 rig; placed via a 0x20 record ONLY in st0202 (Chief's Room window-smash). A distinct, smaller rig from the 20-bone boss but the same animal (cont.23). Other T-Rex appearances are scripted set-pieces (chase/scene), not 0x20 placements.

species.name: Swarm (small dino)
species.note: small swarming dinosaur, simplest skeleton; identical instances share one pointer; rooms 0307/040A/040B. Likely Compsognathus.

species.name: Therizinosaurus
species.note: stage-6 enemy; placed ONLY via the second enemy opcode 0x59 (not 0x20), in rooms 0603/0604/0605/0606/0608. Heaviest skeleton (22 bones). Identified by live spawn + file census (cont.20-22), cross-checked against the wiki enemy for those rooms. LIVE-CONFIRMED in st605 (docs cont.30): two Theri entities (EXE BSS array slots @ 0x6D40C8/0x6D4328) each read entity+0x27=8 (AI category) AND model+0x14=22 (bone count) — both witnesses agree, same dual-witness proof as the cat2 Blue Raptor. The per-frame AI driver (0x46F6FF: mov dl,[ent+0x27]; call [[0x6DE990]+dl*4]) dispatches BOTH Theri to the live cat8 handler 0x0056BFA0 (1512 captured dispatch hits) — so the Theri spawns, animates and is AI-driven (cat8's slot was empty in the static table 0x656E8C; the live stage-6 record 0x655B50 supplies it). Kill-flag = SetFlag(group 4, killid=entity+0x163) into bank 0x6DD990 dword 0x6DD994: killid 53 -> bit21, killid 52 -> bit20 (proven SetFlag math, bank base verified live). Real current-HP field still UNLOCATED (entity body churns every frame; identical outcome to the cat2 raptor cont.26, and HP is not read/written by the randomizer).

_notes: The old 0x20-0x30 'id byte' model is RETIRED: Dino Crisis enemies have no species id byte in the script record (cont.10). Species = the loaded model skeleton. There are TWO enemy-placement opcodes: 0x20 (inline 24-B record) and 0x59 (20-B record; model @ +0x04, motion @ +0x08, pos/kill-flag via a secondary instance table; cont.20). The Tyrannosaurus (both the 20-bone cat-3 boss and the 10-bone cat-4 Chief's-Room rig) is a scripted set-piece and is never randomized — EnemyRecord.IsRandomizableDino excludes it regardless of category, independent of the per-category permute eligibility (cont.23). The randomizer permutes (model,motion) pairs only within one AI category, i.e. only among same-class variants (cont.11/cont.14).
